div1.entryFree: Sanctimony, that which implies the idea of sanctity: if vows be --es, Troil. V, 2, 139. Hence piety and devoutness shown: which holy undertaking with most austere s. she accomplished, All's IV, 3, 59. if s. be the gods' delight, Troil. V, 2, 140. And == outward sanctity, hypocrisy: if s. and a frail vow betwixt an erring barbarian and a supersubtle Venetian be not too hard for my wits, Oth. I, 3, 362.
